title: Royal Challengers Bengaluru: The rise, the heartbreaks and the breakthrough IPL title
author: ET Online
short_desc: Royal Challengers Bengaluru IPL records: RCB achieved their maiden IPL title last season. This victory ended years of near misses for the popular franchise. The team now aims for consistent performance and sustained success. They have assembled a balanced squad for the upcoming season. Off-field cha
long_desc: For years, Royal Challengers Bengaluru were seen as one of the most followed teams in the Indian Premier League without a title. Despite fielding star-studded line-ups and drawing huge crowds, the Bengaluru franchise repeatedly fell short in the final stretch of the tournament.That narrative changed last season when RCB lifted their first IPL trophy, ending a wait that stretched back to the league’s launch in 2008. The win marked a defining moment for a team that had long been labelled as perennial contenders rather than champions.Early years: promise and setbacksRCB began their IPL journey under Rahul Dravid in 2008 but struggled in the inaugural season, finishing seventh out of eight teams with just 4 wins in 14 matches. The franchise quickly rebuilt and emerged as a contender the following year.Also Read: RCB suffer big blow as Nuwan Thushara fails fitness test, denied IPL 2026 NOCIn 2009, a side led by legendary leg-spinner Anil Kumble reached the final. Kumble finished that season as RCB’s leading bowler with 21 wickets at an economy under 6, but the team lost a close contest to Deccan Chargers.RCB returned to the final in 2011, captained by Daniel Vettori, after finishing top of the league table that season. However, they again finished runners-up after losing to Chennai Super Kings.The Kohli era and a historic campaignThe franchise’s identity became closely tied to Virat Kohli, who has represented the team since the first IPL season and remains the highest run-scorer…

title: IPL 2026: Five wicketkeepers to watch out for this season
author: ET Online
short_desc: IPL 2026 teams are focusing on wicketkeepers as key tactical players. MS Dhoni remains vital for CSK. Rishabh Pant anchors LSG's middle order. Quinton de Kock returns to MI as an aggressive opener. Jos Buttler provides top-order firepower for Gujarat Titans. Heinrich Klaasen is a dangerous finisher
long_desc: As the countdown to IPL 2026 begins, team combinations are taking shape, with the wicketkeeper’s role once again emerging as a critical tactical lever—balancing batting depth, flexibility and the Impact Player strategy.Here are five wicketkeepers to watch this season:MS Dhoni (Chennai Super Kings)Also Read: Punjab Kings: IPL’s eternal enigma still chasing its perfect season, a look at how the franchise has done since the first seasonEven at this stage of his career, Dhoni remains central to CSK’s plans. With Sanju Samson joining the franchise, questions remain over who will take the gloves, but Dhoni’s experience and match awareness keep him firmly in focus.Rishabh Pant (Lucknow Super Giants)Pant continues to be among the most influential wicketkeeper-batters in the league. His role at LSG goes beyond keeping, anchoring the middle order while providing explosiveness when required.Quinton de Kock (Mumbai Indians)Back in Mumbai colours for a bargain fee, De Kock is expected to be a first-choice starter. His dual ability as a reliable keeper and aggressive opener makes him a key asset, with youngster Robin Minz as backup.Jos Buttler (Gujarat Titans)One of the most consistent performers in the IPL, Buttler’s value lies in his ability to combine top-order firepower with dependable wicketkeeping, giving his side crucial balance.Heinrich Klaasen (Sunrisers Hyderabad)Klaasen has evolved into one of the most dangerous middle-order hitters in T20 cricket. His presence as a…

title: RCB suffer big blow as Nuwan Thushara fails fitness test, denied IPL 2026 NOC
author: ANI
short_desc: In a disappointing turn of events, Nuwan Thushara has been ruled out of the IPL 2026 opener following an unsuccessful fitness test mandated by Sri Lanka Cricket. This setback means that the Royal Challengers Bengaluru will have to rethink their lineup without him for now. However, Thushara will get
long_desc: Bengaluru: Nuwan Thushara has been ruled out of at least the opening phase of the Indian Premier League (IPL) 2026 after failing to clear a mandatory fitness test conducted by Sri Lanka Cricket, dealing a fresh setback to the defending champions Royal Challengers Bengaluru (RCB).According to a report from ESPNcricinfo, Thushara's failure to meet the required benchmark has resulted in the Sri Lanka board withholding a no-objection certificate (NOC), making him unavailable for immediate participation in the tournament. According to SLC guidelines, players must secure a minimum score of 17 out of 29 across five fitness parameters to be eligible for selection in overseas leagues.Also Read: IPL 2026 Full ScheduleSLC chief executive Ashley de Silva confirmed that the pacer did not achieve the qualifying mark. However, Thushara will have another opportunity to undergo the fitness assessment after a gap of four to five days.The development adds to RCB's growing concerns ahead of their…

title: Punjab Kings: IPL’s eternal enigma still chasing its perfect season, a look at how the franchise has done since the first season
author: ET Online
short_desc: Punjab Kings' IPL story is one of potential and near misses. The team has shown flashes of brilliance but struggled for consistency. Their 2014 season remains a highlight, reaching the final. Despite rebranding, the franchise continues to seek sustained success. Punjab Kings remains an intriguing pa
long_desc: The story of Punjab Kings in the Indian Premier League is one of promise, passion and persistent near-misses — a franchise that has rarely lacked flair, but often fallen short of fulfilment.Born in 2008 as Kings XI Punjab, the team quickly carved out a reputation for unpredictability. In the IPL’s early years, they were a side that could dazzle one evening and unravel the next. With players like Yuvraj Singh leading the charge, Punjab flirted with success but struggled to build sustained momentum in a league that rewards consistency as much as brilliance.Their defining season came in 2014 — a campaign that still lingers as both a high point and a haunting “what if.” Powered by a fearless batting unit led by Glenn Maxwell and anchored by David Miller, Punjab played an exhilarating brand of cricket. They topped the league stage and stormed into the final, only to fall short against Kolkata Knight Riders in a dramatic title clash. It remains their closest brush with the trophy.Since…

title: IPL 2026: CSK all players list. Check out here
author: ET Online
short_desc: Chennai Super Kings stands as a dominant force in the Indian Premier League.  The franchise boasts a history of multiple title wins and consistent performance.  They maintain a strong team by blending seasoned players with emerging talent.  This strategic approach ensures their continued competitive
long_desc: The Chennai Super Kings are one of the most successful and consistent franchises in the Indian Premier League, with multiple title wins and a reputation for stability, experience and strong leadership. Based out of Chennai and playing at the MA Chidambaram Stadium, the side has traditionally relied on a core group of seasoned players while steadily integrating young talent, maintaining a balance that has kept them competitive across seasons.
